mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 3.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations and raises refinancing costs for corporate debt, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 45% of months following PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration of retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses forward earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 689443.0M, HOUST at 1425.0K units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and reduces forward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO is below its 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration of consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals persistent monetary tightening that erodes real equity returns and increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when both inflation and rates are elevated, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in lower inflation and potential Fed rate cuts.
regime_hint: labor market cooling with disinflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during labor market slack periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 20039.406 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or correlation between GDP growth and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Collapse in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million annualized units combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals demand destruction that precedes equity market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprrice recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.2M units AND RSAFS declines MoM
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1425.0K units, RSAFS at 689443.0M
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when both HOUST < 1.2M and RSAFS declines, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.
